1	---
2	name: apple-container
3	description: 'Use when the user mentions apple container, the `container` CLI, migrating from Docker to apple container, or running OCI containers natively on macOS. Also trigger when the user wants to run containers on Mac without Docker Desktop, troubleshoots `container run`/`container build` commands, or discusses launchd-based container orchestration on macOS.'
4	---
5	
6	# Apple Container CLI
7	
8	Apple's native container runtime for macOS. Runs OCI-compatible Linux containers using Apple's virtualization framework ŌĆö no Docker Desktop needed.
9	
10	**Requirements:** macOS 26+ (some networking features require macOS 26)
11	
12	## Install & First-Time Setup
13	
14	```bash
15	brew install container
16	
17	# 1. Start the system service
18	container system start
19	
20	# 2. Install the Linux kernel (required, only once)
21	container system kernel set --recommended
22	
23	# 3. Enable inter-container DNS (required for multi-container setups, needs sudo)
24	sudo container system dns create local
25	container system property set dns.domain local
26	```
27	
28	Step 2 is mandatory ŌĆö without a kernel, `container run` fails. Step 3 requires **both** commands; omitting `property set dns.domain` leaves DNS non-functional even though the domain was created.
29	
30	## Docker ŌåÆ Apple Container Migration
31	
32	The CLI is intentionally Docker-compatible in syntax. Most commands map directly:
33	
34	| Docker                  | Apple Container                             | Notes                                                       |
35	| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
36	| `docker run`            | `container run`                             | Same flags: `-d`, `--rm`, `--name`, `-p`, `-v`, `-e`, `-it` |
37	| `docker build`          | `container build`                           | Same: `-t/--tag`, `-f/--file`, `--build-arg`, `--target`    |
38	| `docker ps`             | `container list` / `container ls`           | `-a` for all                                                |
39	| `docker exec`           | `container exec`                            | Same: `-it`, `-e`, `-w`                                     |
40	| `docker stop`           | `container stop`                            | Adds `--all` flag                                           |
41	| `docker rm`             | `container delete` / `container rm`         | Adds `--all` flag                                           |
42	| `docker logs`           | `container logs`                            | Same: `-f`, `-n`                                            |
43	| `docker images`         | `container image list`                      |                                                             |
44	| `docker pull`           | `container image pull`                      |                                                             |
45	| `docker push`           | `container image push`                      |                                                             |
46	| `docker tag`            | `container image tag`                       |                                                             |
47	| `docker rmi`            | `container image delete`                    |                                                             |
48	| `docker cp`             | `container copy` / `container cp`           |                                                             |
49	| `docker inspect`        | `container inspect`                         |                                                             |
50	| `docker stats`          | `container stats`                           | Adds `--no-stream`                                          |
51	| `docker volume create`  | `container volume create`                   |                                                             |
52	| `docker volume rm`      | `container volume delete`                   |                                                             |
53	| `docker network create` | `container network create`                  | macOS 26+                                                   |
54	| `docker login`          | `container registry login`                  |                                                             |
55	| `docker logout`         | `container registry logout`                 |                                                             |
56	| `docker system prune`   | `container prune` + `container image prune` | Separate commands                                           |
57	| `docker compose`        | _(not available)_                           | No Compose equivalent yet                                   |
58	
59	## Common Workflows
60	
61	### Build and run a web server
62	
63	```bash
64	container build --tag web-app --file Dockerfile .
65	container run --name web-app --detach --rm -p 8080:80 web-app
66	```
67	
68	### Interactive shell in a container
69	
70	```bash
71	container run -it --rm ubuntu:latest /bin/bash
72	# Or exec into running container:
73	container exec -it my-container sh
74	```
75	
76	### Mount volumes
77	
78	```bash
79	# Bind mount
80	container run -v /host/path:/container/path my-image
81	
82	# Named volume
83	container volume create my-data
84	container run -v my-data:/data my-image
85	```
86	
87	### Local DNS (access containers by name)
88	
89	Both commands are required ŌĆö omitting `property set` leaves DNS non-functional:
90	
91	```bash
92	sudo container system dns create local
93	container system property set dns.domain local
94	# Now access containers at: http://<container-name>.local
95	container run --name my-app -d --rm nginx
96	open http://my-app.local
97	```
98	
99	### Push to registry
100	
101	```bash
102	container registry login registry.example.com
103	container image tag my-app registry.example.com/team/my-app:latest
104	container image push registry.example.com/team/my-app:latest
105	```
106	
107	### Resource limits
108	
109	```bash
110	container run --cpus 2 --memory 1024 my-image
111	```
112	
113	### Environment variables
114	
115	```bash
116	container run -e KEY=value --env-file .env my-image
117	```
118	
119	### Networking between containers (macOS 26+)
120	
121	Requires DNS setup first (see Install & First-Time Setup above).
122	
123	```bash
124	container network create my-net
125	container run --name db --network my-net -d postgres
126	container run --name app --network my-net -d my-app
127	# app can reach db at hostname "db" (resolves as db.local)
128	```
129	
130	## Docker Migration Pitfalls
131	
132	### Volumes use ext4 ŌåÆ `lost+found` breaks some images
133	
134	Named volumes are ext4-formatted and contain a `lost+found` directory at the mount root. Images that expect an empty mount point (e.g., PostgreSQL) will fail:
135	
136	```
137	initdb: error: directory "/var/lib/postgresql/data" exists but is not empty
138	```
139	
140	**Fix:** Set the data directory to a subdirectory within the volume:
141	
142	```bash
143	container run -e PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata \
144	  -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres:16-alpine
145	```
146	
147	This applies to any image that checks for an empty mount point (MySQL `datadir`, etc.).
148	
149	### No restart policy ([#286](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/286))
150	
151	Apple Container has no `--restart` flag. Containers do not survive system reboots or crashes. Use a macOS launchd plist with `RunAtLoad` to auto-start containers at login.
152	
153	### No Compose ([Discussion #194](https://github.com/apple/container/discussions/194))
154	
155	No built-in equivalent to `docker compose`. Multi-container orchestration requires a shell script that handles: network/volume creation, startup order, health-check waits, and DSN wiring.
156	
157	### System service doesn't auto-start ([#158](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/158))
158	
159	`container system start` must run before any container commands work. After a reboot, the service is not running. Management scripts should include an `ensure_system` guard:
160	
161	```bash
162	ensure_system() {
163	    if ! container list &>/dev/null; then
164	        container system start 2>/dev/null || true
165	        local i=0
166	        while [ $i -lt 30 ]; do
167	            container list &>/dev/null && return 0
168	            sleep 1; i=$((i + 1))
169	        done
170	        return 1
171	    fi
172	}
173	```
174	
175	### `container inspect` JSON differs from Docker
176	
177	Do not use `grep` to extract fields from `container inspect` output ŌĆö escaping differences (e.g., backslashes in IP addresses) cause subtle bugs. Use proper JSON parsing:
178	
179	```bash
180	# Good
181	container inspect my-container | python3 -c "
182	import sys,json
183	print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['networks'][0]['ipv4Address'].split('/')[0])
184	"
185	
186	# Bad ŌĆö fragile, breaks on escaped characters
187	container inspect my-container | grep -o '"ipv4Address":"[^/]*' | cut -d'"' -f4
188	```
189	
190	### Watchtower / Docker-socket-dependent tools don't work
191	
192	Tools like Watchtower that rely on `/var/run/docker.sock` cannot function in Apple Container. Replace with a launchd plist that periodically runs `container image pull` + recreates the container.
193	
194	### launchd scripts need explicit PATH
195	
196	macOS launchd does not inherit the user's shell PATH. Scripts invoked by launchd must set PATH explicitly, or `container` (installed at `/opt/homebrew/bin/container`) won't be found:
197	
198	```bash
199	#!/bin/bash
200	export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:$PATH"
201	```
202	
203	### `set -euo pipefail` and cleanup commands
204	
205	`container stop` / `container delete` return non-zero for non-existent containers. With `set -e`, this aborts the script. Always append `|| true`:
206	
207	```bash
208	container stop my-app 2>/dev/null || true
209	container delete my-app 2>/dev/null || true
210	```
211	
212	## Key Differences from Docker
213	
214	1. **No daemon** ŌĆö uses macOS launchd services (`container system start/stop`)
215	2. **Apple Virtualization** ŌĆö runs a real Linux VM per container, not a shared daemon
216	3. **No Compose** ŌĆö orchestrate with scripts or use individual commands
217	4. **DNS requires setup** ŌĆö `sudo container system dns create <domain>` + `container system property set dns.domain <domain>`
218	5. **Rosetta support** ŌĆö run x86_64 images on arm64: `container run --rosetta ...`
219	6. **SSH forwarding** ŌĆö `container run --ssh ...` forwards host SSH agent
220	7. **Socket publishing** ŌĆö `container run --publish-socket host:container` for Unix sockets
221	8. **Builder is separate** ŌĆö BuildKit runs in its own container (`container builder start/stop`)
222	
223	## System Management
224	
225	```bash
226	container system status          # Check service health
227	container system version         # Show CLI and API versions
228	container system df              # Disk usage
229	container system logs --last 5m  # Recent service logs
230	container system kernel set --recommended  # Update kernel
231	```
232	
233	## Cleanup
234	
235	```bash
236	container stop --all             # Stop all running containers
237	container delete --all           # Remove all containers
238	container image prune --all      # Remove unused images
239	container volume prune           # Remove unused volumes
240	container network prune          # Remove unused networks
241	```
242	
243	## Further Reference
244	
245	For the complete command reference with all flags and options, fetch:
246	https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apple/container/refs/heads/main/docs/command-reference.md
247

---

# Apple Container CLI

Apple's native container runtime for macOS. Runs OCI-compatible Linux containers using Apple's virtualization framework ŌĆö no Docker Desktop needed.

**Requirements:** macOS 26+ (some networking features require macOS 26)

## Install & First-Time Setup

```bash
brew install container

# 1. Start the system service
container system start

# 2. Install the Linux kernel (required, only once)
container system kernel set --recommended

# 3. Enable inter-container DNS (required for multi-container setups, needs sudo)
sudo container system dns create local
container system property set dns.domain local
```

Step 2 is mandatory ŌĆö without a kernel, `container run` fails. Step 3 requires **both** commands; omitting `property set dns.domain` leaves DNS non-functional even though the domain was created.

## Docker ŌåÆ Apple Container Migration

The CLI is intentionally Docker-compatible in syntax. Most commands map directly:

| Docker                  | Apple Container                             | Notes                                                       |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `docker run`            | `container run`                             | Same flags: `-d`, `--rm`, `--name`, `-p`, `-v`, `-e`, `-it` |
| `docker build`          | `container build`                           | Same: `-t/--tag`, `-f/--file`, `--build-arg`, `--target`    |
| `docker ps`             | `container list` / `container ls`           | `-a` for all                                                |
| `docker exec`           | `container exec`                            | Same: `-it`, `-e`, `-w`                                     |
| `docker stop`           | `container stop`                            | Adds `--all` flag                                           |
| `docker rm`             | `container delete` / `container rm`         | Adds `--all` flag                                           |
| `docker logs`           | `container logs`                            | Same: `-f`, `-n`                                            |
| `docker images`         | `container image list`                      |                                                             |
| `docker pull`           | `container image pull`                      |                                                             |
| `docker push`           | `container image push`                      |                                                             |
| `docker tag`            | `container image tag`                       |                                                             |
| `docker rmi`            | `container image delete`                    |                                                             |
| `docker cp`             | `container copy` / `container cp`           |                                                             |
| `docker inspect`        | `container inspect`                         |                                                             |
| `docker stats`          | `container stats`                           | Adds `--no-stream`                                          |
| `docker volume create`  | `container volume create`                   |                                                             |
| `docker volume rm`      | `container volume delete`                   |                                                             |
| `docker network create` | `container network create`                  | macOS 26+                                                   |
| `docker login`          | `container registry login`                  |                                                             |
| `docker logout`         | `container registry logout`                 |                                                             |
| `docker system prune`   | `container prune` + `container image prune` | Separate commands                                           |
| `docker compose`        | _(not available)_                           | No Compose equivalent yet                                   |

## Common Workflows

### Build and run a web server

```bash
container build --tag web-app --file Dockerfile .
container run --name web-app --detach --rm -p 8080:80 web-app
```

### Interactive shell in a container

```bash
container run -it --rm ubuntu:latest /bin/bash
# Or exec into running container:
container exec -it my-container sh
```

### Mount volumes

```bash
# Bind mount
container run -v /host/path:/container/path my-image

# Named volume
container volume create my-data
container run -v my-data:/data my-image
```

### Local DNS (access containers by name)

Both commands are required ŌĆö omitting `property set` leaves DNS non-functional:

```bash
sudo container system dns create local
container system property set dns.domain local
# Now access containers at: http://<container-name>.local
container run --name my-app -d --rm nginx
open http://my-app.local
```

### Push to registry

```bash
container registry login registry.example.com
container image tag my-app registry.example.com/team/my-app:latest
container image push registry.example.com/team/my-app:latest
```

### Resource limits

```bash
container run --cpus 2 --memory 1024 my-image
```

### Environment variables

```bash
container run -e KEY=value --env-file .env my-image
```

### Networking between containers (macOS 26+)

Requires DNS setup first (see Install & First-Time Setup above).

```bash
container network create my-net
container run --name db --network my-net -d postgres
container run --name app --network my-net -d my-app
# app can reach db at hostname "db" (resolves as db.local)
```

## Docker Migration Pitfalls

### Volumes use ext4 ŌåÆ `lost+found` breaks some images

Named volumes are ext4-formatted and contain a `lost+found` directory at the mount root. Images that expect an empty mount point (e.g., PostgreSQL) will fail:

```
initdb: error: directory "/var/lib/postgresql/data" exists but is not empty
```

**Fix:** Set the data directory to a subdirectory within the volume:

```bash
container run -e PGDATA=/var/lib/postgresql/data/pgdata \
  -v pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres:16-alpine
```

This applies to any image that checks for an empty mount point (MySQL `datadir`, etc.).

### No restart policy ([#286](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/286))

Apple Container has no `--restart` flag. Containers do not survive system reboots or crashes. Use a macOS launchd plist with `RunAtLoad` to auto-start containers at login.

### No Compose ([Discussion #194](https://github.com/apple/container/discussions/194))

No built-in equivalent to `docker compose`. Multi-container orchestration requires a shell script that handles: network/volume creation, startup order, health-check waits, and DSN wiring.

### System service doesn't auto-start ([#158](https://github.com/apple/container/issues/158))

`container system start` must run before any container commands work. After a reboot, the service is not running. Management scripts should include an `ensure_system` guard:

```bash
ensure_system() {
    if ! container list &>/dev/null; then
        container system start 2>/dev/null || true
        local i=0
        while [ $i -lt 30 ]; do
            container list &>/dev/null && return 0
            sleep 1; i=$((i + 1))
        done
        return 1
    fi
}
```

### `container inspect` JSON differs from Docker

Do not use `grep` to extract fields from `container inspect` output ŌĆö escaping differences (e.g., backslashes in IP addresses) cause subtle bugs. Use proper JSON parsing:

```bash
# Good
container inspect my-container | python3 -c "
import sys,json
print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['networks'][0]['ipv4Address'].split('/')[0])
"

# Bad ŌĆö fragile, breaks on escaped characters
container inspect my-container | grep -o '"ipv4Address":"[^/]*' | cut -d'"' -f4
```

### Watchtower / Docker-socket-dependent tools don't work

Tools like Watchtower that rely on `/var/run/docker.sock` cannot function in Apple Container. Replace with a launchd plist that periodically runs `container image pull` + recreates the container.

### launchd scripts need explicit PATH

macOS launchd does not inherit the user's shell PATH. Scripts invoked by launchd must set PATH explicitly, or `container` (installed at `/opt/homebrew/bin/container`) won't be found:

```bash
#!/bin/bash
export PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin:$PATH"
```

### `set -euo pipefail` and cleanup commands

`container stop` / `container delete` return non-zero for non-existent containers. With `set -e`, this aborts the script. Always append `|| true`:

```bash
container stop my-app 2>/dev/null || true
container delete my-app 2>/dev/null || true
```

## Key Differences from Docker

1. **No daemon** ŌĆö uses macOS launchd services (`container system start/stop`)
2. **Apple Virtualization** ŌĆö runs a real Linux VM per container, not a shared daemon
3. **No Compose** ŌĆö orchestrate with scripts or use individual commands
4. **DNS requires setup** ŌĆö `sudo container system dns create <domain>` + `container system property set dns.domain <domain>`
5. **Rosetta support** ŌĆö run x86_64 images on arm64: `container run --rosetta ...`
6. **SSH forwarding** ŌĆö `container run --ssh ...` forwards host SSH agent
7. **Socket publishing** ŌĆö `container run --publish-socket host:container` for Unix sockets
8. **Builder is separate** ŌĆö BuildKit runs in its own container (`container builder start/stop`)

## System Management

```bash
container system status          # Check service health
container system version         # Show CLI and API versions
container system df              # Disk usage
container system logs --last 5m  # Recent service logs
container system kernel set --recommended  # Update kernel
```

## Cleanup

```bash
container stop --all             # Stop all running containers
container delete --all           # Remove all containers
container image prune --all      # Remove unused images
container volume prune           # Remove unused volumes
container network prune          # Remove unused networks
```

## Further Reference

For the complete command reference with all flags and options, fetch:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/apple/container/refs/heads/main/docs/command-reference.md

# Apple Container Release Summary

## Latest Release: Version 1.0.0 (June 9, 2024)

The most recent release marks the project's first anniversary with significant features and breaking changes:

### Key Features in 1.0.0:
- **`container machine`** for long-lived Linux environments with tight host integration
- **TOML configuration file** replaces UserDefault-backed system properties
- **`container cp` command** for host-container file transfer
- Cleaned up structured output (JSON, YAML, TOML) formatting
- XPC-based IP address leak fixes for networking

### Previous Notable Releases:

**0.12.3** (April 30, 2024) - Security patch including "prevent HTTP downgrade in registry-related commands" and DNS command injection prevention

**0.12.0** (April 27, 2024) - Added plain/color progress output, YAML format support, Linux capabilities support (`--cap-add`/`--cap-drop`), and zstd compression support

**0.11.0** (March 31, 2024) - Introduced `container export`, build secrets, Dockerfile-specific ignore files, and MTU network options

### Breaking Changes:
The 1.0.0 release includes "removing the `container system property` `get` and `set` subcommands" and reorganization of API structures for better plugin support.

# DNS Configuration in Container

Based on the documentation, DNS configuration is handled through the **TOML configuration file** at `~/.config/container/config.toml`, not via command-line property setting.

## Current Procedure

To set the default DNS domain, edit your configuration file:

```toml
[dns]
domain = "test"
```

For setting up local DNS and enabling host service access from containers, use the command-line approach:

```bash
sudo container system dns create host.container.internal --localhost 203.0.113.113
```

The documentation notes that this creates "a DNS domain with `--localhost <ipv4-address>` to make a domain used by a container to access a host service."

The system properties list shows that DNS configuration includes a `domain` setting under the `[dns]` section, confirming the TOML file is the primary configuration method for default domain setup.

# Container System Configuration Guide

## Config File Path

The user configuration file is located at `~/.config/container/config.toml`. Create the directory structure with:
```
mkdir -p ~/.config/container
touch ~/.config/container/config.toml
```

## DNS Domain Configuration

To configure the DNS domain, add this section to your TOML file:

```toml
[dns]
domain = "test"
```

## System Properties and TOML Mapping

The documentation shows these top-level configuration sections in TOML:

- **[build]** - Builder configuration (CPUs, memory, Rosetta, image)
- **[container]** - Container defaults (CPUs, memory)
- **[dns]** - DNS settings (domain)
- **[kernel]** - Kernel configuration (binary path, URL)
- **[network]** - Network settings
- **[registry]** - Registry configuration (domain)
- **[vminit]** - Virtual machine initialization (image)

As stated in the guide: *"Each top-level table maps directly to a section of ContainerSystemConfig."*

## Verification

After restarting the service (`container system stop` then `container system start`), verify configuration with:
```
container system property list
```

For JSON output, use the `--format json` flag.

**Requirements:** macOS 26+ (some networking features require macOS 26)

## Install & First-Time Setup

```bash
brew install container

# 1. Start the system service
container system start

# 2. Install the Linux kernel (required, only once)
container system kernel set --recommended

# 3. Enable inter-container DNS (required for multi-container setups, needs sudo)
sudo container system dns create local
container system property set dns.domain local
```

Step 2 is mandatory ŌĆö without a kernel, `container run` fails. Step 3 requires **both** commands; omitting `property set dns.domain` leaves DNS non-functional even though the domain was created.
input.new_string:
**Requirements:** macOS 26 only. Apple does not support older macOS versions (it depends on macOS 26 virtualization/networking features).

> **Version note (1.0.0+):** `container system property get`/`set` were **removed**. System configuration now lives in a TOML file at `~/.config/container/config.toml`. The DNS setup below reflects this; older docs showing `container system property set dns.domain ...` are outdated and will fail.

## Install & First-Time Setup

```bash
brew install container

# 1. Start the system service
container system start

# 2. Install the Linux kernel (required, only once)
container system kernel set --recommended

# 3. Enable inter-container DNS (required for multi-container setups, needs sudo)
sudo container system dns create local

# 4. Set it as the default domain via the TOML config (replaces the old `property set`)
mkdir -p ~/.config/container
cat >> ~/.config/container/config.toml <<'EOF'
[dns]
domain = "local"
EOF

# 5. Restart the service so the config takes effect
container system stop && container system start
```

Step 2 is mandatory ŌĆö without a kernel, `container run` fails. For DNS you need **both** the `dns create` command (step 3) and the `[dns] domain` entry in `config.toml` (step 4); creating the domain alone leaves resolution non-functional. Verify config with `container system property list`.
